title: Candidate-window LambdaRank reranker on top of the current classifier
summary: ROC AUC is a pairwise ranking metric, and ranking objectives are designed to optimize pair ordering directly.
rationale: ROC AUC is a pairwise ranking metric, and ranking objectives are designed to optimize pair ordering directly. A small reranker focused on ambiguous candidate laps can improve ordering near true stop windows without replacing the current strong classifier.
implementation_hint: Keep the current best classifier as stage 1. Build query groups by `Race_Year` + `Driver`, restrict stage 2 to plausible stop-window laps or high-uncertainty laps, and train XGBoost `rank:pairwise`/`rank:ndcg` or LightGBM `lambdarank` on out-of-fold stage-1 features plus race-state features; blend the rank score back into the final probability.
expected_effect: Should lift AUC by correcting near-miss ordering around actual stop laps, where most of the remaining error likely lives.
risk: If groups are poorly chosen or positives are too sparse, the reranker will overfit; OOF generation and race-blocked grouping are mandatory.
